title: How to build an AI Agent and MCP Server (step-by-step)
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that enables AI agents to connect securely with real-world live data and tools. Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) provides tools to build AI agents using MCP, demonstrated with a use case connecting Google Trends to a blog writing agent.
writeup: This walkthrough builds an AI agent and an MCP server step-by-step using Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK), with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) as the connective standard. MCP is an open standard that lets agents connect securely to live real-world data and tools, which matters for grounding agents in current information rather than static knowledge. The concrete example connects Google Trends to a blog-writing agent, showing how a tool source feeds an agent task. The video also covers multi-agent systems alongside the single-agent build.
